translation.en: I feel lazy and don't want to do anything.

content.sentence: Tôi cảm thấy ___ không muốn làm gì.

notes_md:
**lười** = lazy; not wanting to do something because it feels like too much effort.

It’s an adjective, used to describe a person’s attitude toward actions (work, studying, cleaning, etc.). Very often followed by a verb to say what someone is too lazy to do.  

You can also say **lười biếng** for a slightly stronger or more judgmental “lazy.”  
Opposite: **chăm chỉ** (hardworking) — a useful contrast to remember.

content.sentence: Hôm nay tôi rất ___ để học.

translation.en: Today I am very lazy to study.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Today I feel very lazy so I don't want to do anything.
He is often lazy to get up early on the weekend.
You shouldn't be lazy if you want good results at work.

example.es: Soy muy perezoso por la mañana.
example.vi: Tôi rất lười biếng vào buổi sáng.
example.en: I am very lazy in the morning.

definitions.es: Reacio a trabajar o usar energía; perezoso.
definitions.vi: Không muốn làm việc hoặc sử dụng năng lượng; lười biếng.
definitions.en: Unwilling to work or use energy; lazy.

notes:
lười = lazy; not wanting to do something because it feels like too much effort.

It’s an adjective, used to describe a person’s attitude toward actions (work, studying, cleaning, etc.). Very often followed by a verb to say what someone is too lazy to do.  

You can also say lười biếng for a slightly stronger or more judgmental “lazy.”  
Opposite: chăm chỉ (hardworking) — a useful contrast to remember.
exampleSentences: Hôm nay em cảm thấy rất lười nên không muốn làm gì cả.
Anh ấy thường lười dậy sớm vào cuối tuần.
Chị không nên lười nếu muốn đạt kết quả tốt trong công việc.
